content.full_text:
air Political Practices CommissioIL 
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814 
Florence Bernstein 
Attention: 
Deborah Stimming 
9595 Wilshire Blvd. 
Suite 711 
Beverly Hills, CA 90212 
Dear Ms. Stimming: 
May 18, 1976 
This letter confirms our telephone conversation of May 17, 
1976, concerning invitations to an art show, auction and brunch 
honoring Florence Bernstein, a candidate for municipal judge. 
Each invitation is a single piece of paper imprinted with the 
auctioneer's name, street address, and city as the ~eturn address. 
The auctioneer supplied 1,000 invitations, and the Florence 
Bernstein committee purchased an additional 2,000. 
The committee 
mailed approximately 2,000 invitations using first class postage. 
The mailing is a mass mailing, subject to the requirements 
of Government Code Section 84305. 
However, the committee has 
substantially complied with the requirements of that section by 
including on the outside of the mailing the name and address of 
a person who paid for a significant portion of the printing 
costs. 
The committee should report the costs of the mailing as 
expenditures on its campaign statements. 
In addition, you should 
report the fair market value of the invitations donated by the 
auctioneer as an in-kind contribution, unless there are additional 
facts showing that the auctioneer received full and adequate 
consideration for his payment. 
I hope this information will be of assistance. 
NEW:av 
Sincerely, 
. 
. (

The committee should report the costs of the mailing as expenditures on its campaign statements. The fair market value of the invitations donated by the auctioneer should be reported as an in-kind contribution, unless there are additional facts showing that the auctioneer received full and adequate consideration for his payment.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a candidate committee's mass mailing of invitations to an art show, auction, and brunch for Florence Bernstein (candidate for municipal judge) complies with Government Code Section 84305 requirements, and how to report the costs and donated invitations.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The mass mailing substantially complies with Government Code Section 84305 requirements. Mailing costs must be reported as campaign expenditures, and the fair market value of donated invitations should be reported as an in-kind contribution unless the auctioneer received full consideration.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION section headers present in document. Both question and conclusion synthesized from body text of response letter. Clear FPPC advice letter with substantive legal guidance on compliance and reporting requirements.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a candidate committee's mass mailing of invitations to an art show, auction, and brunch for Florence Bernstein (candidate for municipal judge) complies with Government Code Section 84305 requirements, and how to report the costs and donated invitations.

CONCLUSION: The committee has substantially complied with the requirements of Government Code Section 84305 by including on the outside of the mailing the name and address of a person who paid for a significant portion of the printing costs. The committee should report the costs of the mailing as expenditures on its campaign statements. The fair market value of the invitations donated by the auctioneer should be reported as an in-kind contribution, unless there are additional facts showing that the auctioneer received full and adequate consideration for his payment.

embedding.summary: FPPC staff counsel confirms that a candidate committee's mass mailing of invitations for a fundraising art show, auction, and brunch substantially complies with Government Code Section 84305 and provides guidance on reporting the mailing costs and in-kind contributions.
